<!-- Topic 6: Fill In the Missing Code #2 -->
<!-- Total slides: 3 -->

# Fill In the Missing Code #2

---

## Exercise: dynamic array — allocate, populate, clean up {.smaller}

+ The program reads a count from the user, allocates a dynamic array of that size, fills it, and prints the maximum value.
+ Fill in the missing block to complete the allocation and cleanup.

::: notes
Total slides: 3
Students need to write: new int[n], the population loop, delete[] arr, arr = nullptr. About 5 lines in a contiguous block.
:::

<!-- Slide 1 -->

---

## The code — fill in lines 11–16 {.smaller}

```{.cpp}
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int findMax(const int* arr, int n) {
    int max = arr[0];
    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++)
        if (arr[i] > max) max = arr[i];
    return max;
}

int main() {
    int n;
    cout << "How many values? ";
    cin >> n;

    int* arr;

    // === YOUR CODE HERE (lines 16–21) ===
    // Allocate a dynamic int array of size n.
    // Read n integers from the user into the array.
    // =====================================

    cout << "Max: " << findMax(arr, n) << "\n";

    // === YOUR CODE HERE (lines 25–26) ===
    // Release the array and null the pointer.
    // =====================================

    return 0;
}
```

<!-- Slide 2 -->

---

## Solution {.smaller}

```{.cpp}
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int findMax(const int* arr, int n) {
    int max = arr[0];
    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++)
        if (arr[i] > max) max = arr[i];
    return max;
}

int main() {
    int n;
    cout << "How many values? ";
    cin >> n;

    int* arr;

    arr = new int[n];
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        cout << "Value " << (i + 1) << ": ";
        cin >> arr[i];
    }

    cout << "Max: " << findMax(arr, n) << "\n";

    delete[] arr;
    arr = nullptr;

    return 0;
}
```

<!-- Slide 3 -->
